rulings.Case Title: MARY C. MCFADDEN VS. MARY L. MCFADDEN, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 4, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Enforce Settlement is GRANTED. Defendant is in material breach of the settlement agreement. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's Request for Attorney's Fees is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The October 25, 2018 Settlement Agreement states, "If any party to this Settlement Agreement commits a material breach of the terms of this Settlement Agreement (as determined by the San Francisco Superior Court)[,] then the prevailing party [may] enforce the terms of this Settlement Agreement [and recover] their reasonable attorney fees and costs of enforcing the Settlement Agreement. This Court has found Defendant to have committed a material breach of the settlement agreement. Accordingly, reasonable attorney's fees are granted to Plaintiff in the amount of $13,005 (fees incurred in direct connection with the "enforcement" of the agreement; all other fees and costs in the amount of $6,810 were not found to have been incurred in direct connection with the "enforcement" of the agreement). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE S. DORFMAN VS. RICHARD CARTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Plaintiff?S Motion And Motion To Appoint Partition Referee Per Ccp 873.010
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 4, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE DORFMAN'S RE-NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPOINT PARTITION REFERREE PER CCP 873.010 is GRANTED. The parties complied with this Court's prior December 2, 2021, order and have stipulated to appoint Amy Harrington, Esq., as referee. Accordingly, Amy Harrington, Esq., is appointed partition referee per CCP 873.010. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. EILEEN L. WALSH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 4, 2022 line 4. RECEIVER'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY is GRANTED, no opposition filed. Pursuant to this Court's Order Appointing Receiver, dated September 13, 2021, the Receiver shall have the power and the responsibility to "take full and complete possession and control of the [Subject] Property . . . [t]o temporarily or permanently relocate the Property's occupants [] if necessary to implement the Receivership Plan, as determined by the Receiver in its sole and absolute discretion." Accordingly, per the September 13, 2021 Order, Receiver may temporarily vacate and dispossess Defendant from the Subject property for the amount of time that is necessary for him to remediate that property's numerous violations and deficiencies. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JANE ECKERT VS. ERIC ROSENQUIST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 4, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT ERIC ROSENQUIST'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly allege facts that state a cause of action for forcible detainer, including whether the complaint is brought under CCP Section 1160(a)(1) or 1160(a)(2), and how Defendant has used force or threats of force against Plaintiff's attempt to regain possession. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
